env.col.name: Name
env.note.machine: System variables — editing requires running as administrator
env.note.removeAdmin: Save failed: editing system variables requires running as administrator.
env.note.saved: Saved. Newly opened programs / terminals will pick up the change.
env.subtitle: View / edit user or system environment variables. Changes take effect for programs and terminals opened afterwards.
env.title: Environment Variables
maint.clean.busyBody: Cleaning the {0} selected items of cache and temporary files; files in use are skipped automatically.
maint.clean.confirm:
Clean the {0} selected items, about {1}?

Files in use are skipped automatically.
maint.event.none: No critical / error events in the last 7 days
maint.event.note: Click “Refresh” to view critical / error events from the last 7 days
maint.event.summary: {0} critical/error events in the last 7 days
maint.events.title: Recent Errors / Crashes
maint.junk.empty: No obvious junk files found to clean
maint.junk.item.windows-old.detail: The system kept from the last upgrade
maint.junk.note: Click “Scan” to estimate clearable cache and temporary files
maint.junk.title: Junk Cleanup
maint.repair.cancelled: Administrator authorization (UAC) was cancelled, or launch failed
maint.repair.cmdDone: ===== Done, you can close this window =====
maint.repair.item.chkdsk.detail: Read-only check of the system drive for errors (add /f to repair, requires reboot): chkdsk C:
maint.repair.item.chkdsk.title: Disk Check (chkdsk C:)
maint.repair.item.dism-clean.detail: Clean up superseded components and shrink WinSxS: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /StartComponentCleanup
maint.repair.item.dism-clean.title: DISM Clean Component Store
maint.repair.item.dism.detail: Repair the Windows component store: DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth
maint.repair.item.dism.title: DISM Repair System Image
maint.repair.item.flushdns.title: Flush DNS Cache
maint.repair.item.gpupdate.title: Refresh Group Policy
maint.repair.item.icon-cache.detail: Delete the icon cache and restart Explorer (the desktop will flash once)
maint.repair.item.icon-cache.title: Rebuild Icon Cache
maint.repair.item.net-reset.detail: Flush DNS + reset Winsock + reset TCP/IP (a reboot is recommended afterwards)
maint.repair.item.net-reset.title: Reset Network Stack
maint.repair.item.sfc.detail: Scan and repair corrupted system files: sfc /scannow
maint.repair.item.sfc.title: System File Checker (SFC)
maint.repair.item.wu-cache.detail: Stop service → clear SoftwareDistribution\Download → restart service
maint.repair.item.wu-cache.title: Clear Windows Update Cache
maint.repair.title: One-Click Repair
maint.subtitle: Common system repair commands, junk cleanup, and recent error/crash events — the repair technician's bench. Items marked “Admin required” trigger a UAC prompt.
maint.title: Maintenance
proc.endAll.title: End all processes
proc.endOne.title: End process
proc.expandAll.tip: Expand / collapse the sub-processes of every group at once
proc.headerTip: Left-click to expand / collapse sub-processes; right-click to end all / restart / open file location / properties
proc.subtitle: Live (every 2 s) CPU usage and memory per process, with expand/collapse and end single/all. “Show all processes” partitions into user / system sections (like Task Manager); right-click a process to end task / open file location / properties.
proc.summary.all: All processes: {0} user · {1} system · {2} processes total · refreshes every 2 s
proc.summary.allEmpty: No software from the library is running (click “Show all processes” to see everything)
proc.summary.lib: {0} app(s) running · {1} processes total · refreshes every 2 s
proc.title: Process Manager
proc.toggle.tip: Switch between “all processes” and “library processes only”

startup.remove.title: Delete startup item
startup.subtitle: Manage Windows startup entries (registry Run keys + Startup folders): enable / disable / delete. HKLM and common startup items require running as administrator.
startup.summary.idle: Click Refresh to scan startup items
startup.title: Startup Items
startup.toggle.fail:

sysov.gpu.label: Graphics
sysov.adminRun.tip: Restart as administrator to read CPU temperature and CPU/board power (AMD MSR/SMU via the WinRing0 driver, requires admin).
sysov.cpu.needAdminTemp: ·  administrator rights needed to read temperature
sysov.disks.title: Disk Space
sysov.eject.busyFallback: The device is in use and cannot be ejected.
sysov.eject.forceFail: Force-eject failed; close the programs using the device and try again.
sysov.eject.forced: The device was force-ejected and can now be safely removed.
sysov.eject.noIndex: Could not determine the physical disk number for this device.
sysov.eject.notFound: Device not found; it may have already been removed.
sysov.eject.success: The device was safely ejected and can now be removed.
sysov.eject.veto.cmError: The Configuration Manager returned an error (0x{0}).
sysov.eject.veto.default: The device is in use and cannot be ejected.
sysov.eject.veto.device: The device is currently busy and cannot be ejected.
sysov.eject.veto.driver: The device driver blocked the eject.
sysov.eject.veto.insufficientRights: Insufficient rights to eject this device.
sysov.eject.veto.outstandingOpen: A program still has a file or folder open on the device.
sysov.eject.veto.pendingClose: The device is closing; please wait and retry.
sysov.eject.veto.windowsApp: An application is using the device.
sysov.eject.veto.windowsService: A Windows service is using the device.
sysov.ejectDlg.busy.body: You can try “Force eject”: this immediately unmounts all partitions on the device and disconnects it. In-progress or unsaved reads/writes may be lost, so confirm nothing important is running first.
sysov.ejectDlg.fail.title: Cannot eject device
sysov.ejectDlg.notBusy.body: Close the programs or Explorer windows accessing the device and try again.
sysov.ejectDlg.success.title: Safely ejected
sysov.export.title: Export Software Inventory
sysov.mem.label: Memory
sysov.mem.live: Used {0} GB  ·  Available {1} GB  ·  Total {2} GB
sysov.power.note.estimate: Total power is an estimated sum of component draws (excluding the board / fans and other sensor-less parts).
sysov.power.note.noCpu: Excludes CPU / board power: reading them requires running as administrator (AMD reads MSR/SMU via WinRing0); GPU power is read via NVAPI without admin. Most drives expose no power sensor.
sysov.power.note.noDriver: The hardware-monitor driver failed to load (power / temperature unavailable).
sysov.power.note.noSensorAdmin: No power sensor detected. Some boards / GPUs / laptops provide no power sensor.
sysov.power.note.noSensorUser: No power sensor detected. Reading CPU package power requires running this program as administrator.
sysov.powerTip.adaptive: About {0}% of peak power (reference cap ~{1} W, peak-adaptive)
sysov.powerTip.exact: About {0}% of the power cap (cap {1} W, from nvidia-smi)
sysov.smart.attr.name: Attribute
sysov.smart.attrTable.title: SMART Attributes
sysov.smart.banner.highRisk: ⚠ High-risk items detected (reallocated / pending / uncorrectable sectors > 0). Back up your data as soon as possible.
sysov.smart.heading: SMART / disk health diagnostics
sysov.smart.note.elevated: This drive / controller exposes no SMART attributes (some USB / RAID / NVMe bridge controllers don't support standard ATA SMART reads).
sysov.smart.note.external: SMART for USB / external drives can only be read after running this program as administrator (via SCSI / ATA pass-through).
sysov.smart.note.local: Temperature / lifespan / SMART attributes require administrator rights to read (HDD C5/C6, SSD read/write totals, etc.).
sysov.smart.note.noAttr: No SMART attributes obtained (authorization cancelled, or this controller/drive doesn't support ATA SMART, such as some NVMe / USB / RAID).
sysov.smart.tip: Click to view this disk's SMART / reliability data
sysov.smart.title: Physical Disk Health (SMART) · click a disk for details
sysov.subtitle: A live one-glance look at hardware / system health: CPU, memory, power, disks (incl. SMART), battery, activation status (refreshes every 2 s).
sysov.title: System Overview
